title: Pricing Overview
text:
# BrightDesk Pricing

BrightDesk offers three plans to fit your team's needs.

## Starter Plan
$12 per user per month (billed annually) or $15 per user per month (billed monthly). Includes up to 5 team members, 1,000 tickets per month, email support, and basic reporting.

## Professional Plan
$29 per user per month (billed annually) or $35 per user per month (billed monthly). Includes unlimited team members, unlimited tickets, priority support, advanced reporting, and API access.

## Enterprise Plan
Custom pricing. Includes everything in Professional plus SSO, dedicated account manager, custom integrations, SLA guarantees, and on-premise deployment option.

All plans include a 14-day free trial. No credit card required.

title: Sales One-Pager
text:
# BrightDesk for Your Team

BrightDesk is the #1 customer support platform for growing teams.

## Why BrightDesk?

Faster response times. Happier customers. Less busywork.

## Plans

Starter: $15/user/month. Up to 10 users, 2,000 tickets/month, email support.

Professional: $35/user/month. Unlimited users and tickets, phone and email support, analytics dashboard.

Enterprise: Contact sales. Everything in Pro plus SSO, dedicated CSM, and custom SLAs.

## Customers Love Us

4.8 stars on G2. Trusted by 5,000+ companies worldwide.

## Get Started

Book a demo at brightdesk.com/demo or start your free trial today.

title: Feature List
text:
# BrightDesk Features

## Ticket Management
Multi-channel inbox (email, chat, social media). Automatic ticket routing and prioritization. Custom ticket fields and workflows. SLA tracking and alerts.

## Knowledge Base
Public-facing help center. Internal knowledge base for agents. AI-powered article suggestions. Full-text search with filters.

## Reporting
Real-time dashboard with key metrics. Custom report builder. Scheduled report delivery via email. Export to CSV and PDF.

## Integrations
Slack, Jira, Salesforce, HubSpot, Zapier. REST API for custom integrations. Webhook support for real-time events.

## Security
SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. Two-factor authentication. Role-based access control. Data encryption at rest and in transit.

title: Feature Comparison by Plan
text:
# Feature Comparison

## Starter
Email inbox only. Basic ticket routing. Up to 5 custom fields. Standard reports. Email support (48-hour response time).

## Professional
Multi-channel inbox (email, chat, social). Advanced routing with AI. Unlimited custom fields. Custom report builder. Priority support (4-hour response time). API access. Slack and Jira integrations.

## Enterprise
Everything in Professional. SSO (SAML/OAuth). Dedicated account manager. Custom integrations. 99.9% uptime SLA. On-premise deployment. Phone support (1-hour response time). Audit logs.

title: Setup Guide
text:
# Getting Started with BrightDesk

## Step 1: Create Your Account
Sign up at brightdesk.com/signup. You'll need a work email address. All new accounts start with a 14-day free trial of the Professional plan.

## Step 2: Invite Your Team
Go to Settings > Team > Invite Members. Enter email addresses for your team. They'll receive an invitation to join your workspace.

## Step 3: Connect Your Email
Go to Settings > Channels > Email. Add your support email address (e.g., support@yourcompany.com). BrightDesk will automatically import incoming emails as tickets.

## Step 4: Customize Your Workflow
Set up ticket categories, priorities, and assignment rules. Configure your SLA policies. Customize your ticket form fields.

## Step 5: Go Live
Update your support email forwarding to point to BrightDesk. Publish your knowledge base. Share your new help center URL with customers.

title: Onboarding Guide
text:
# BrightDesk Onboarding

## Create Your Account
Visit brightdesk.com/signup and register with your work email. New accounts get a 14-day free trial of the Professional plan with full access to all features.

## Add Team Members
Navigate to Settings > Team > Invite Members. Add your team by entering their email addresses. Each person will get an email invitation to join.

## Set Up Email Channel
In Settings > Channels > Email, add your support address (like support@yourcompany.com). Incoming emails will automatically create tickets in your inbox.

## Configure Workflows
Customize ticket categories, set priority levels, and create auto-assignment rules. Define SLA policies for different ticket types. Add custom fields to your ticket form.

## Launch
Forward your support email to BrightDesk. Publish your help center. Share the help center link with your customers.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
text:
# FAQ

## How much does BrightDesk cost?
Our Starter plan begins at $12/user/month when billed annually. The Professional plan is $29/user/month annually. Enterprise pricing is custom. All plans include a 14-day free trial.

## Can I change plans later?
Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade at any time. Changes take effect at the start of your next billing cycle.

## What payment methods do you accept?
We accept all major credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, Amex) and invoicing for annual Enterprise contracts.

## Is there a free trial?
Yes, all plans come with a 14-day free trial. No credit card required to start.

## How many users can I have?
Starter supports up to 5 users. Professional and Enterprise have no user limit.

## Do you offer phone support?
Phone support is available on Enterprise plans only. Starter and Professional plans include email and chat support.

title: Setting Up SLA Policies
text:
# How to Set Up SLA Policies

SLA (Service Level Agreement) policies help you track and meet response time commitments.

## Creating an SLA Policy

1. Go to Settings > SLAs > New Policy
2. Name your policy (e.g., 'Premium Support SLA')
3. Set response time targets for each priority level:
   - Urgent: 1 hour first response
   - High: 4 hours first response
   - Normal: 8 hours first response
   - Low: 24 hours first response
4. Set resolution time targets
5. Choose which tickets this policy applies to (by tag, customer tier, or channel)

## SLA Alerts

BrightDesk automatically alerts agents when an SLA is approaching breach. Alerts are sent 15 minutes before the deadline via email and in-app notification.

## SLA Reporting

Track SLA compliance in Settings > Reports > SLA Dashboard. See breach rates by team, agent, priority, and time period.

title: API Documentation (v1)
text:
# BrightDesk API v1 (Deprecated)

Note: API v1 was deprecated in January 2024. Please use API v2.

Base URL: https://api.brightdesk.com/v1

Authentication: Basic auth with your API key.

## Endpoints

GET /tickets - List tickets (max 50 per page)
POST /tickets - Create a ticket
GET /tickets/:id - Get a ticket
PUT /tickets/:id - Update a ticket

Rate limit: 60 requests per minute.

For API v2 documentation, see docs.brightdesk.com/api/v2.
